title: aster divaricatus var. chlorolepis
intro: Aster divaricatus var. chlorolepis, also known as the white wood aster, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its white or pale purple flowers and divided leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Aster divaricatus var. chlorolepis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using its roots and rhizomes to create a decoction, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
